sections.paragraph:
λύκος, ου, ὁ (Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, Test12Patr; JosAs 12:10; SibOr 8, 41; 13, 28; Philo; Just., A I, 58, 2; loanw. in rabb., but s. KRengstorf, ELittmann Festschr., ’35, 55–62)

① wolf, lit., as a symbol Mt 10:16; Lk 10:3; J 10:12ab; D 16:3 (in all these pass. in contrast to sheep; cp. Dio Chrys. 64 [14], 2; Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. 8, 22; Philo, Praem. 86; Just., A I, 58, 2; Iren. 1, Prologue 2 [Harv. I 4, 3]; Did., Gen. 86, 18).

② a fierce or vicious pers., wolf metaph., ext. of mng. 1 (in imagery as early as Il. 4, 471; 16, 156; Epict. 1, 3, 7 al.; cp. Zeph 3:3; Jer 5:6; Ez 22:27; Rhodon [in Eus., HE 5, 13, 4] refers to Marcion as ὁ Ποντικὸς λύκος), of evil people IPhld 2:2; 2 Cl 5:2–4 (fr. a noncanonical gospel). λ. ἅρπαγες ravenous wolves Mt 7:15. λ. βαρεῖς fierce wolves Ac 20:29.—B. 185. DELG. M-M. TW. Spicq.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

ext. ext. = extension

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

  1. fr. = from
  2. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 604
